note: Descriptive map of where each country's observable activity sits across the semiconductor value chain. This is an information map, not a ranking or score.

cells.Malaysia: No 12-inch fab; in planning / investment-promotion stage.
cells.Vietnam: No front-end fab; front-end supply chain nearly blank.
cells.Thailand: No front-end fab; front-end / power devices at planning stage.
cells.Indonesia: No fab; silica-downstreaming wafer roadmap 2025–2035 just starting.

cells.Malaysia: Mature OSAT; ~13% of global back-end capacity; extending to advanced packaging & substrates.
cells.Thailand: Back-end present; Infineon advanced-packaging fab under construction.
cells.Philippines: Veteran ATP base (73% SMS / 27% EMS); world's 9th largest chip exporter.

layers.description: Chip design houses and design-services activity.

cells.Malaysia: Emerging; Penang design push (e.g., Oppstar); extending from back-end.
cells.Vietnam: Fastest-rising in region; IC design firms ~40 → ~60 in two years; ~7,000 designers.
cells.Indonesia: Nascent; ARM×Danantara training targeting design talent.

layers.description: Consumables, specialty gases, tools, local supply-chain depth.

cells.Malaysia: Back-end consumables/equipment co-located in Penang; front-end specialty materials imported.
cells.Vietnam: ~15 foreign material/equipment suppliers; front-end materials mostly imported.
cells.Thailand: Deep PCB / power-device material supply chain; front-end wafer materials to be built.
cells.Indonesia: Weak; electronic-grade processing infrastructure a key challenge.
cells.Philippines: ATP consumables present; high-end materials/equipment import-dependent.

layers.description: Electronics manufacturing services and downstream demand clusters.

cells.Malaysia: Broad E&E base; ~600,000 workers in E&E.
cells.Vietnam: Large FDI electronics base (Samsung, etc.); strong assembly gravity.
cells.Thailand: PCB/HDD global leader; automotive & electronics clusters (EEC).
cells.Philippines: Electronics ~60% of exports; EMS base (IMI, etc.).

layers.description: National strategies, incentives, and talent-training programs.

cells.Malaysia: National Semiconductor Strategy; target 60,000 trained by 2030.
cells.Vietnam: National semiconductor talent target 50,000–100,000 by 2030; STEM intake scaling.
cells.Thailand: 4 national semiconductor training labs (KMITL); EEC incentives; target 100,000 high-skill.
cells.Indonesia: SKKNI 130/2024 standards; ARM×Danantara 15,000-engineer program; US$150M state investment.
cells.Philippines: Upskill target ~128,000 by 2028–2030; English-language workforce advantage.
